reviews.author: Tasha (AutismFamilyTravelGuide)
reviews.text: Back Beach (Bãi Sau) is Vũng Tàu’s main beach and a great place for a relaxed walk along the coast. The beach is wide and sandy, with plenty of space to wander, fly kites, or simply enjoy the sea breeze, especially in the late afternoon when the heat starts to ease. The promenade behind the beach is lively with cafés, food stalls, hotels, and ice-cream spots, and there are shaded areas and seating along the way. It’s not the quietest beach in Vietnam, but that’s part of its charm — it feels energetic, local, and full of life. Swimming conditions vary depending on the day, but for strolling, people-watching, and catching the sunset over the water, Back Beach is a lovely place to spend an hour or two while exploring Vũng Tàu. We were lucky to go on a day when the kids could play in the water. It was so warm!

reviews.author: Hoàng Nguyễn Xuân
reviews.text: Beautiful place. Old Flag Pole Square just has an magnificent bronze whale statue erected. Whales are revered by the locals as the God of Nam Hải (Southern Sea), and a marble slab is carved explaining this custom. (I propose this square to be renamed the Square of Nam Hải =)))

reviews.author: Steven
reviews.text: Super nice beach. Clean, salty water, what else can you ask for. Chairs are 100k (50k each) Change rooms/washrooms 20k each person (just change outside if you’re a guy) Parking 10k

reviews.author: Paolo Bevilacqua
reviews.text: Big wide beach, great for relaxing and walking on the sand. Swimming is ok though lots of flags out for dangerous surf when we were there and also a bit of rubbish around. Chairs, umbrellas and showers all available with a small fee.

reviews.author: Khoa Dang
reviews.text: Visited the back beach of VT. It was less crowded and spacious. Decent tides. The water was seaweed-ish color but good enough I felt. Several public toilet and shower facilities at approximately 1km-1.5km away. Local vendors were very respectful. We came in 5 ppl, rented 3 chairs, did not order drinks and sellers just let us enjoy ourselves. The only drawback is all the water pumps along the shore did not work at all.

reviews.author: The Genesis Family
reviews.text: Many dead squid, I wonder why. Near by is having construction and the beach is filled with litter. Seems a great spot for local families. It's a long, sandy stretch perfect for kids to play and build sandcastles. The waves are generally gentle, making it safe for a swim thou I read from some review to not venture out too far out as the waves and currents are strong there. While it can get busy on weekends, especially with locals, there are plenty of food stalls and hotels nearby. Don't expect pristine, clear waters like some remote islands, but it's a convenient and lively option for a beach day close to the city.

reviews.author: Thomas KIM
reviews.text: Wonderful and fine sand of the beach...you can walk in the sea, not too deep from the seaside.

reviews.author: Lydia Chu
reviews.text: Back Beach in Vung Tau is a peaceful spot to relax and enjoy the ocean. There was hardly anybody there when I visited because of the rain, which made it even more serene. The beach is clean, and there’s plenty of space to walk, swim, or just enjoy the view. I also noticed the small shrine on an island nearby and the impressive Christ statue overlooking the area. It’s a beautiful place to unwind, with some unique sights to explore. Definitely worth a visit, especially if you like a quieter beach experience!

reviews.author: Francesco Betti
reviews.text: The beach is not yet fully accessible, as extensive coastal development work is underway. For example, showers are currently unavailable (there are only large water containers with ladles). The beach has great potential because it appears larger than the front beach, and the gardens behind it, once completed, will likely be even more beautiful.

reviews.author: John Strachan
reviews.text: Nice long beach, fairly crowded at weekends. Trash is not binned, a lot of plastics lying and floating about, including diapers (nappies) You wade through trash in the water. Better in mornings or evenings, very hot through the day. Hotel private areas, clean and well stocked with nice furniture, no trash on these parts.

reviews.author: Stephen Merrett
reviews.text: More commercialised than Front Beach but a far better and more accessible beach area. A lot of the foreshore area is being developed making access a bit difficult but most likely better in the medium term

reviews.author: Adnan Zahoor
reviews.text: Awesome beach, the water was great in December 2024! The kids loved playing in the sand. I wish we could've stayed longer. That park across the street was nice too.

reviews.author: Xiêm Bùi Thị
reviews.text: Back Beach in Vung Tau is an ideal destination with a long, clean sandy beach and clear blue sea. The fresh air and gentle waves make it perfect for swimming and relaxing walks. The surrounding area offers many restaurants and comfortable hotels, making it well worth a visit.

reviews.author: Geoff Holland
reviews.text: The five stars is in anticipation of when the redevelopment of Back Beach finally finishes. What they have done with the tower and the parklands around it is beautiful and intelligently designed. At the moment the area is a bit scrappy because of the redevelopment of the beach itself, along with buildings having been torn down and Construction that will go on for some time.

reviews.author: admen Lim
reviews.text: Nice beach but crowded Lots of seafood along the costal

reviews.author: Alma Atla
reviews.text: Good and nice beach. Better go there from 5:30 am till 7:30am or after 5pm. Too hot during the day. At you can see the fishermen coming back from their morning fishing trip.

reviews.author: Edward Harrison
reviews.text: As opposed to the front beach the back beach offers a wider scale of natural sea Beauty l, at night this place flourishing with young people and romantic couples and family trips

reviews.author: FOLLOW ME.
reviews.text: I've spent years in and out of this place. Litteraly, know this place like the back of my hand. Much better now that all the Beg packers have gone.. Covid 19. Was certainly a good way to clean out the trash. Definitely need to clean up the beach. It's littered with Litter.. Filthy Animals..,

reviews.author: Tuncay Sonay
reviews.text: The biggest beach around however no sunset seen from that point. Still convenient location for short time touristic visits, since it's surrounded with variety of food options

reviews.author: ManNie TT
reviews.text: Kind of ok to visit this beach! Not that bad like I've read the reviews. I went on the weekend and not so crowded. The cheapest motels here are started from 500,000 vn₫.

reviews.author: Tamara Kasalica
reviews.text: We travelled 2.5 hours hoping for a refreshing and scenic outing — but sadly, the visit didn’t live up to expectations. Although the setting initially looked promising, upon arrival we discovered that the water was unswimmable, and a black flag was already raised, indicating caution.

reviews.author: Geoff Edwards
reviews.text: So much better than a few years ago.... Really clean and accessible walkways... Beutiful walks and amazing beach...

reviews.author: Hien Nguyen
reviews.text: I visited Vũng Tàu just two days ago, and I must say I was truly surprised by how much the city has changed compared to my last trip two years ago. Back then, Vũng Tàu felt quieter, with fewer options for dining and entertainment. This time, however, the city seemed full of life, energy, and excitement. The beaches were much more crowded, filled with both locals and tourists enjoying the sea breeze and the waves. While I usually prefer calm and peaceful beaches, I found the lively atmosphere here refreshing and enjoyable. It felt as though Vũng Tàu had become a more dynamic destination, attracting more visitors than ever before. What impressed me the most was the variety and quality of restaurants and cafés. Two years ago, choices were limited, but now I found so many modern and stylish places offering delicious food, fresh seafood, and even international cuisine. Many of these spots had beautiful designs and great service, making the dining experience even more memorable. Walking through the streets, I could feel a stronger sense of hospitality and professionalism from local businesses. Overall, Vũng Tàu left me with a very positive impression this time. The city has grown into a more vibrant and welcoming destination while still keeping its natural charm and beautiful coastline. I was happy to see such improvements, and I believe Vũng Tàu will continue to develop into one of the most attractive seaside cities in Vietnam.

reviews.author: Sam Truong
reviews.text: The Back Beach of Vũng Tàu is a nice place to relax and enjoy the view. Decent crowd size, but not overwhelming. There's an island off to the right or south side with Hòn Bà Temple on it. You can find pretty cool shells lying on the beach sand too!

reviews.author: Dawn Hale
reviews.text: A continuous beautiful walking beach. Hard packed sand loves bare feet, warm water and places to rent shade.

reviews.author: Zhui Ri
reviews.text: I have to say that I was and am highly disappointed in this beach; there is garbage everywhere! There are wrappers, masks, diapers, plastic cups, pieces of paper, plastic bags, plastic utensils, and just about every kind of garbage imaginable here. It's a shame too, because it's not terribly crowded and there's room to enjoy it if there wasn't trash on the beach and in the water, but just walking into the water makes you feel like you're in a hot garbage soup. I felt incredibly dirty with diapers washing into the shore. I tried to take a fun photo of my feet in the water and a wave pushed a mask right into the shot; an all too common sight: garbage in the water.

reviews.author: ajay shakya
reviews.text: It was a very beautiful sightseeing with amazing feelings after visiting this place.

reviews.author: Robin Chandra Chakravorty
reviews.text: Beautiful beach with not many visitors from Monday to Fridays ,life guards and first aid are in place

reviews.author: Hung Duong Phuoc
reviews.text: Let's try anytime you can, book a homestay near the beach with an affordable price to down your accommodation expense, so that, you & your partners will have more chances to enjoy the seafood, street food & smoothies.

reviews.author: Mike Perry
reviews.text: Fantastic food, great location and reasonable price.
